heading: Application
label: 10301 Application
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource section (U.S. Code)1030146:56446:56646:574Section 10301 specifies that vessels on foreign and intercoastal voyages (except for fishing vessels and foreign vessels) are subject to the seamen protection and relief provisions contained in chapter 103.

Amendments2006—Subsec. (b). inserted “or to riding gang members” after “voyage”.Pub. L. 109–2411996—Subsec. (a)(2). inserted “as measured under , or an alternate tonnage measured under as prescribed by the Secretary under ” after “75 gross tons”.Pub. children.text: on a voyage between a port in the United States and a port in a foreign country (except a port in Canada, Mexico, or the West Indies); or

of at least 75 gross tons as measured under , or an alternate tonnage measured under as prescribed by the Secretary under on a voyage between a port of the United States on the Atlantic Ocean and a port of the United States on the Pacific Ocean.section 14502 of this titlesection 14302 of this titlesection 14104 of this title

children.label: b
children.text: This chapter does not apply to a vessel on which the seamen are entitled by custom or agreement to share in the profit or result of a voyage or to riding gang members.

children.label: c
children.text: Unless otherwise provided, this chapter does not apply to a foreign vessel.
